---
title: "Figure4"
author: "Emanuelle Grody"
date: "2026-03-26"
output: html_document
---

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
source("~/SALVEseq/extractionScripts/R/packages.R")
source("~/SALVEseq/extractionScripts/R/functions.R")
```

## Panel B
```{r}
base.dir <- "/projects/b1042/GoyalLab/egrody/extractedData/EGS024/barcode/stepThree/"
singlet.dir <- "/projects/b1042/GoyalLab/egrody/extractedData/EGS024/barcode/stepFour/"

singlet_1 <- read.csv(paste0(singlet.dir, "GFP_barcode_1__singlets_all.txt"), col.names = "cellID")
singlet_9 <- read.csv(paste0(singlet.dir, "GFP_barcode_9__singlets_all.txt"), col.names = "cellID")
singlet_data <- data.frame(cellID = union(singlet_1$cellID, singlet_9$cellID)) %>%
  mutate(cellID = paste0(cellID, "-1"))

results <- list()
plot_list <- list()

for (threshold in 0:5) {
  barcodeData <- list()
  for (i in side_reactions) {
    temp <- read.delim(file.path(base.dir, i, "stepThreeStarcodeShavedReads.txt"))
    barcodeData[[i]] <- temp %>% mutate(cellID = paste0(cellID, "-1")) %>%
      select(cellID, UMI, BC50StarcodeD8) %>%
      filter(cellID %in% singlet_data$cellID) %>%
      filter(cellID %in% UMAP_data$cellID) %>%
      count(cellID, BC50StarcodeD8, name = "count") %>%
      filter(count > threshold)
  }

  lib1 <- barcodeData[[1]] %>%
    count(BC50StarcodeD8, name = "lib1_abundance")
  lib2 <- barcodeData[[2]] %>%
    count(BC50StarcodeD8, name = "lib2_abundance")
  abundance <- inner_join(lib1, lib2, by = "BC50StarcodeD8")

  r_squared <- cor(abundance$lib1_abundance, abundance$lib2_abundance)^2

   p <- ggplot(abundance, aes(x = lib2_abundance, y = lib1_abundance)) +
    geom_bin2d() +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("black", "blue", "purple", "red", "yellow"),
                         name = "Number of\nBarcodes",
                         trans = "log10") +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    scale_x_log10() +
    scale_y_log10() +
    annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.5,
             label = paste0("R^2 == ", round(r_squared, 3)), parse = TRUE) +
    labs(x = "Barcode abundance\nlibrary 2",
         y = "Barcode abundance\nlibrary 1",
         title = paste0("threshold > ", threshold)) +
    coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 10) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

  plot_list[[as.character(threshold)]] <- p
}

# Arrange all threshold plots in a single row
combined <- wrap_plots(plot_list, nrow = 1)

output.dir <- "/projects/b1042/GoyalLab/egrody/extractedData/EGS024/barcode/"
ggsave(paste0(output.dir, "barcode_overlap_thresholds.pdf"), combined,
       width = 4 * length(plot_list), height = 4)
```




## Panel C
```{r}
sample_name <- "GFP_invitro"
combined.dir <- "/projects/b1042/GoyalLab/egrody/extractedData/EGS024/combined/"
combined_data <- read.csv(paste0(combined.dir, "dataframes/", sample_name, "_all.csv"))

plotBarcodeUMAP(combined_data, "GFP_invitro clones", min_count = 2, 
                output_dir = paste0(combined.dir, "plots/"),
                saveas = paste0("UMAP_barcode_clones.pdf"))
plotBarcodeUMAP(combined_data, "GFP_invitro singletons", max_count = 1, 
                output_dir = paste0(combined.dir, "plots/"),
                saveas = paste0("UMAP_barcode_singletons.pdf"))
plotBarcodeUMAP(combined_data, "GFP_invitro clones sized > 50", min_count = 50, 
                output_dir = paste0(combined.dir, "plots/"),
                saveas = paste0("UMAP_barcode_clonesGreaterThan50.pdf"))


# identity UMAP: barcoded and infected
plot_data <- combined_data
plot_data$highlight <- ifelse(plot_data$barcode != "none" & plot_data$total > 0, "yes", "no")
plot_data <- arrange(plot_data, highlight)

p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = UMAP1, y = UMAP2, color = highlight)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = highlight)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("no" = "gray93", "yes" = "black")) +
  scale_size_manual(values = c("no" = 0.5, "yes" = 0.8)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  guides(size = "none", color = "none") +
  ggtitle("Barcoded cells with nonzero total expression")

print(p)
ggsave(p, file = paste0(combined.dir, "plots/UMAP_barcoded_infected.pdf"),
       device = "pdf")

# clone fate: infected proportion of clone
plot_data <- combined_data
plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  filter(barcode != "none") %>%
  mutate(infected = ifelse(total > 0, "infected", "uninfected"))
clone_counts <- plot_data %>%
  group_by(barcode) %>%
  summarise(
    clone_size = n(),
    n_infected = sum(infected == "infected"),
    n_uninfected = sum(infected == "uninfected"),
    pct_infected = round(100 * n_infected / clone_size, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_infected), desc(clone_size))

cat("Total infected cells:", sum(clone_counts$n_infected), "\n")
cat("Total clones with >= 1 infected cell:",
    sum(clone_counts$n_infected > 0), "of", nrow(clone_counts), "\n\n")

cat("Per-clone breakdown:\n")
for (i in seq_len(nrow(clone_counts))) {
  row <- clone_counts[i, ]
  cat(sprintf("  %s | infected: %d | uninfected: %d | %s%%\n",
              row$barcode, row$n_infected, row$n_uninfected, row$pct_infected))
}

# Plot: only clones with >= 1 infected cell
infected_clones <- clone_counts %>% filter(n_infected > 0)

if (nrow(infected_clones) > 0) {
  infected_clones$label <- substr(infected_clones$barcode, 1, 10)
  infected_clones$label <- factor(infected_clones$label,
                                   levels = rev(infected_clones$label))

  infected_long <- infected_clones %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c(n_infected, n_uninfected),
                        names_to = "status", values_to = "count") %>%
    mutate(status = ifelse(status == "n_infected", "infected", "uninfected"))

  p <- ggplot(infected_long, aes(x = label, y = count, fill = status)) +
    geom_col() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("infected" = "black", "uninfected" = "gray80")) +
    coord_flip() +
    theme_classic() +
    labs(x = "", y = "Number of cells in clone", fill = "",
         title = "Clones with infected cells")

  print(p)
}
```

## Panel D
For Cluster 1
```{r}
#cluster1 <- subset(seurat_obj, 
#                     barcode_status %in% c("singleton", "clone"))
#
# uncomment the above to run for all clusters
#
cluster1 <- subset(seurat_obj, seurat_clusters == 1 & 
                     barcode_status %in% c("singleton", "clone"))
pca <- Embeddings(cluster1, "pca")[, 1:30]
meta_c1 <- cluster1@meta.data
is_sing <- meta_c1$is_singleton

sing_idx <- which(is_sing)
clone_idx <- which(!is_sing)

set.seed(42)
n_pairs <- 2000  # consistent across all comparisons

pair_dist <- function(pairs, pca) {
  sqrt(rowSums((pca[pairs[,1],] - pca[pairs[,2],])^2))
}

# 1. Within-clone: both cells share the same barcode
clone_barcodes <- meta_c1$barcode[clone_idx]
# Only barcodes with 2+ cells in this cluster
bc_counts <- table(clone_barcodes)
eligible_bcs <- names(bc_counts[bc_counts >= 2])

within_pairs <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_pairs, ncol = 2)
for (k in seq_len(n_pairs)) {
  bc <- sample(eligible_bcs, 1)
  members <- clone_idx[clone_barcodes == bc]
  picked <- sample(members, 2, replace = FALSE)
  within_pairs[k,] <- picked
}
d_within <- pair_dist(within_pairs, pca)

# 2. Between-clone: two clone cells with different barcodes
between_pairs <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_pairs, ncol = 2)
for (k in seq_len(n_pairs)) {
  i <- sample(clone_idx, 1)
  j <- sample(clone_idx, 1)
  while (meta_c1$barcode[j] == meta_c1$barcode[i]) {
    j <- sample(clone_idx, 1)
  }
  between_pairs[k,] <- c(i, j)
}
d_between <- pair_dist(between_pairs, pca)

# 3. Singleton-Singleton
ss_pairs <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_pairs, ncol = 2)
for (k in seq_len(n_pairs)) {
  picked <- sample(sing_idx, 2, replace = TRUE)
  while (picked[1] == picked[2]) {
    picked <- sample(sing_idx, 2, replace = TRUE)
  }
  ss_pairs[k,] <- picked
}
d_ss <- pair_dist(ss_pairs, pca)

# 4. Clone-Singleton
cs_pairs <- cbind(
  sample(clone_idx, n_pairs, replace = TRUE),
  sample(sing_idx, n_pairs, replace = TRUE)
)
d_cs <- pair_dist(cs_pairs, pca)

# 5. Random (any two cells in cluster 1)
all_idx <- seq_len(nrow(pca))
rand_pairs <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_pairs, ncol = 2)
for (k in seq_len(n_pairs)) {
  picked <- sample(all_idx, 2)
  rand_pairs[k,] <- picked
}
d_rand <- pair_dist(rand_pairs, pca)

# Summary
cat("Within-clone:       ", round(mean(d_within), 3), "+/-", round(sd(d_within), 3), "\n")
cat("Between-clone:      ", round(mean(d_between), 3), "+/-", round(sd(d_between), 3), "\n")
cat("Singleton-Singleton:", round(mean(d_ss), 3), "+/-", round(sd(d_ss), 3), "\n")
cat("Clone-Singleton:    ", round(mean(d_cs), 3), "+/-", round(sd(d_cs), 3), "\n")
cat("Random:             ", round(mean(d_rand), 3), "+/-", round(sd(d_rand), 3), "\n")

# Plot
dist_df <- data.frame(
  distance = c(d_within, d_between, d_ss, d_cs, d_rand),
  comparison = rep(c("Within-clone", "Between-clone", 
                      "Singleton-Singleton", "Clone-Singleton", "Random"),
                   each = n_pairs)
)
dist_df$comparison <- factor(dist_df$comparison,
                              levels = c("Within-clone", "Between-clone",
                                         "Singleton-Singleton", 
                                         "Clone-Singleton", "Random"))


library(ggpubr)

comparisons_list <- list(
  c("Within-clone", "Between-clone"),
  c("Within-clone", "Singleton-Singleton"),
  c("Within-clone", "Clone-Singleton"),
  c("Within-clone", "Random"),
  c("Between-clone", "Singleton-Singleton"),
  c("Between-clone", "Clone-Singleton"),
  c("Singleton-Singleton", "Clone-Singleton"),
  c("Singleton-Singleton", "Random")
)

p <- ggplot(dist_df, aes(x = comparison, y = distance)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "gray80") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1) +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = comparisons_list, 
                     method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.signif",
                     step.increase = 0.05) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = "", y = "Euclidean distance (PCA space)") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))
output_dir <- "/projects/b1042/GoyalLab/egrody/extractedData/EGS024/joint/plots/"
ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "barcode_edist_violins_cluster1.pdf"), p)
```
